name: From Landais to the Abbey of Sordes - loop
name_original: De Landais à l'Abbaye de Sordes - boucle
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Dax are characterized by diverse landscapes, ranging from flat coastal paths through maritime pine forests to undulating terrain in the Chalosse region. The area features the Adour River Valley with its unique "Barthes" water meadows, alongside numerous lakes and an extensive network of dedicated cycle paths. This region offers varied road cycling experiences across its natural features, with elevations generally remaining below 200 meters. Best road cycling routes around Dax The most popular road cycling route is Lakes of Léon and Soustons Loop, a 34.1 miles (54.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route combines lakeside paths with sections through forests. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Adour Valley - La Scandibérique Loop, an easy 27.1 miles (43.7 km) path. This route offers picturesque cycling opportunities along the banks of the Adour River. Local road cyclists also love the Crossing the bridges of the Adour, a 35.2 miles (56.7 km) trail leading through the Adour River Valley, often completed in about 2 hours 18 minutes. Road cycling around Dax is defined by extensive pine forests, the Adour River Valley, and rolling hills in the Chalosse region. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Dax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 1,500 reviews. More than 33,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Dax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Dax?
A: The region around Dax offers a diverse range of terrains. You'll find long, flat stretches along the Atlantic coast, often shaded by maritime pine forests, ideal for relaxed rides. The Adour River Valley features picturesque routes with a mix of smooth and occasionally unpaved surfaces. To the east and south, the Chalosse region presents rolling hills with undulating terrain and short, sharp climbs for more athletic cyclists.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy ride?
A: Yes, Dax offers numerous easy road cycling routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938727545/adour-valley-la-scandiberique-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adour Valley - La Scandibérique Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 43.7 km path offering picturesque cycling along the Adour River. The extensive network of cycle paths, including sections of the Vélodyssée, also provides many flat and accessible options.

Q: Where can I find more challenging road cycling routes near Dax?
A: For more challenging rides, head towards the Chalosse region, east and south of Dax. This area is characterized by rolling hills and offers routes with undulating terrain and short, sharp climbs. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938889554/saint-paul-les-dex-to-saint-sever-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint-Paul-lès-Dex to Saint-Sever - loop&lt;/a&gt;, covering over 115 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around Dax often pass through stunning natural landscapes. You can explore the vast Landes forest of maritime pines, cycle along the banks of the Adour River and its unique 'Barthes' water meadows, or enjoy tranquil rides around lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3467910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;West Lake of Soustons&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3551855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Léon Lake&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Courant d'Huchet Nature Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, offering diverse scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Dax are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938731781/lakes-of-leon-and-soustons-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lakes of Léon and Soustons Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which combines lakeside paths with forest sections, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949486671/landes-sweets-cycling-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landes Sweets Cycling Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 92 km ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Dax?
A: The region is generally pleasant for cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, making them ideal. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal routes benefit from sea breezes. The extensive pine forests also provide shade on warmer days. Winter cycling is possible, though cooler and potentially wetter.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes along the Adour River Valley offer picturesque views, often passing through charming villages and belvederes. The coastal routes provide scenic vistas of the Atlantic, dunes, and lakes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e949457274/crossing-the-bridges-of-the-adour" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crossing the bridges of the Adour&lt;/a&gt; route offers varied riverine landscapes.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Dax area?
A: There are over 1,400 road cycling routes available around Dax, catering to various skill levels. This includes a significant number of easy and moderate routes, alongside more challenging options for experienced cyclists.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Dax?
A: The road cycling routes around Dax are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 1,500 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from the tranquil pine forests and scenic lakes to the picturesque Adour River Valley, as well as the extensive and well-maintained network of cycle paths.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling near Dax?
A: Yes, Dax is situated near major European cycling routes like the Vélodyssée (EuroVélo 1) and EuroVélo 3. These routes provide access to long-distance touring possibilities, allowing cyclists to explore beyond the immediate Dax area and connect to other regions of France.

Q: Can I find cafes or pubs along the cycling routes in Dax?
A: The region features routes that pass through picturesque villages, offering opportunities to experience local heritage and find amenities. The 'Accueil Vélo' label, prevalent in the area, indicates establishments that offer services like secure bike parking and useful information, often including cafes and accommodation, making it easier to plan stops along your ride.

Q: What is the typical elevation gain on road cycling routes around Dax?
A: While the Chalosse region offers undulating terrain, elevations generally remain below 200 meters for many routes. However, some longer or more challenging routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e965313430/from-landais-to-the-abbey-of-sordes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Landais to the Abbey of Sordes - loop&lt;/a&gt;, can feature elevation gains exceeding 600 meters, providing a good workout.

Sordes Abbey, in the town of Sorde-l'Abbaye, is a Benedictine abbey from the 10th century.
